user_query:
I'm organizing our next community testing sprint for Atari 2600 emulator compatibility. Please use the three provided inputs to standardize the test plan and summarize current results.

Inputs to use (do not modify):
- input/docs/test_plan.md (an outdated test plan)
- input/game_catalog.json (games and hardware features)
- input/compatibility_log.csv (executed test results)

Deliverables:
1) Rewrite the test plan and save to output/test_plan.md with these requirements:
   - Retain the exact top-level title line from input/docs/test_plan.md as the first line of the new file.
   - Replace any existing test listing with a generated "Test Matrix" that enumerates ONLY games where priority is true in input/game_catalog.json.
   - For each priority game, include the required tests derived from its hardware:
     - Always include FRAME and RESET.
     - Include BANK if bank_switching != "none".
     - Include PADDLE if controller == "paddle".
     - Include DRIVE if controller == "driving".
   - Use canonical test IDs: T-{game_id}-{CODE}, where CODE is one of [FRAME, RESET, BANK, PADDLE, DRIVE].
   - Next to each test_id, include the test name using these exact CODE-to-name mappings:
     - FRAME = "Frame timing stability"
     - RESET = "Reset/Start response"
     - BANK = "Bank-switching stability"
     - PADDLE = "Paddle centering & jitter"
     - DRIVE = "Driving controller quadrature"
   - The matrix must list the game title, game_id, and each required test_id with its mapped name. Keep formatting readable but you do not need to match any specific table style.

2) Compose a status report and save to output/status_report.md with these sections:
   - "Pass/Fail by Emulator Version": for each (emulator, version) pair found in input/compatibility_log.csv, report total_tests, pass, fail, and pass_rate (0–100, rounded to two decimals). Compute strictly from the CSV.
   - "Open Failures": list every failing row from input/compatibility_log.csv as bullet points that include emulator, version, game_id, title, test_id, and notes.
   - "Coverage Gaps": list any required test_ids (as defined by the rewritten plan and hardware rules above) that have no matching row in input/compatibility_log.csv. Show items as "game_id test_id missing".

3) Write an aggregated CSV to output/compatibility_summary.csv with columns: emulator,version,total_tests,pass,fail,pass_rate. Compute pass_rate as a numeric percentage from 0 to 100 rounded to two decimals (no percent sign).

Constraints & notes:
- Derive the set of required tests from input/game_catalog.json using the rules above, and ensure test_id strings match exactly the T-{game_id}-{CODE} pattern so they can be cross-checked with input/compatibility_log.csv.
- Use the emulator and version strings exactly as they appear in input/compatibility_log.csv.
- Do not introduce games not marked priority:true, and do not modify the input files.
- All counts and rates in the report and CSV must be recomputable from input/compatibility_log.csv.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
